natural_sources: As a trace element in seawater
natural_occurrence.description: Gold is a precious metal that occurs naturally in pure metallic form. It is chemically inert and does not corrode. Food-grade gold is extremely pure (typically 22-24 karat) and hammered into ultra-thin sheets.

manufacturing.description: Produced by refining gold from ores or recycled sources, then hammered into extremely thin sheets (gold leaf, typically 0.1-0.2 micrometers thick) or ground into fine powder or flakes. Food-grade gold must be at least 90% pure (22 karat), preferably 99.9% (24 karat).

other_uses.cosmetics: Used in luxury skincare products, face masks, and cosmetics for anti-aging claims and aesthetic appeal.
other_uses.industrial: Used in electronics, aerospace, dentistry, and as a conductor in various high-tech applications.
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Used in some medications for rheumatoid arthritis (gold salts) and in dental restorations.
other_uses.household: Gold jewelry, decorative items, and investment.

eu.conditions: Only for external decoration of confectionery, chocolate products, and liqueurs. Decorative use only.

usa.classification: Generally Recognized As Safe (GRAS) for decorative purposes
usa.notes: Approved only for external decoration, not for general consumption. Must be pure gold.

japan.notes: Used traditionally in premium Japanese confectionery and sake

canada.notes: Permitted for surface decoration of confectionery and alcoholic beverages

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: Not specified (gold is chemically inert; virtually no absorption)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: Not specified (negligible exposure; use restricted to external decoration)

regulatory_history.event: EFSA confirms safety of gold for external decoration at current use levels

food_categories_eu.category_name: Decorations, coatings and fillings (external decoration only)

commonly_found_in: Ultra-luxury chocolates and truffles (24k gold leaf)
Premium liqueurs (Goldschläger, Danziger Goldwasser with gold flakes)
Premium sushi and Japanese cuisine garnish
